categories.title: Organization Profile
categories.description: Tell us about your organization so we can understand your scale and context.

options.label: Professional firm (law, accounting, healthcare, financial)
Technology company or startup
Nonprofit, advocacy, or journalism organization
Other small business or organization

questions.text: How many people rely on your technology day-to-day?

options.label: More than 50

questions.text: What type of data does your organization handle?

options.label: General business data (internal docs, email, scheduling)
Regulated data (legal, health, financial records)
Highly sensitive (PII, attorney-client, medical records)
Not sure what category our data falls into

categories.title: Current Infrastructure
categories.description: Understanding what you have today helps us design what comes next.

questions.text: Where does your infrastructure live today?

options.label: Everything is in the cloud (Google, Microsoft, AWS, etc.)
We have on-premises servers or equipment
Mix of cloud services and on-premises equipment
We don't really have formal infrastructure

questions.text: How many distinct services or applications does your team use daily?

options.label: Under 5
More than 15

questions.text: Do you currently have an IT provider or managed service provider (MSP)?

options.label: No — we handle IT ourselves or don't have formal IT support
Yes, but we're not satisfied with the service
Yes, and the relationship is working well
We have an internal IT person or team

questions.text: What is your biggest frustration with your current IT setup?

options.label: No documentation — nobody knows the full picture
We're not confident our backups would actually work
Slow or unresponsive support from our current provider
We feel locked into a vendor or platform we can't easily leave
We don't know how secure our infrastructure really is
Things are mostly working fine

categories.title: Security Posture
categories.description: Security is built into everything we do. These questions help us understand your current posture and needs.

questions.text: Have you experienced a security incident, data loss, or extended outage in the past two years?

options.label: Yes — it caused significant disruption or data loss
Yes — but it was minor or quickly resolved
No
We're not sure — we might not have detected one

questions.text: If your primary server or service failed right now, how confident are you in recovery?

options.label: Very confident — we have tested backups and a documented plan
Somewhat — we have backups but haven't tested recovery
Not confident — backups may exist but we don't know the state
We don't have reliable backups

questions.text: Do you have compliance or regulatory requirements for your infrastructure?

options.label: Yes — specific framework (HIPAA, SOC 2, PCI-DSS, etc.)
Yes — general best-practice expectations from clients or partners
No formal requirements
Not sure

questions.text: How important is data sovereignty — keeping data on infrastructure you own and control?

options.label: Critical — it's a core organizational value or requirement
Important — we'd prefer it but can be flexible
Nice to have but not a priority
Not important — we're comfortable with cloud storage

categories.title: Availability and Recovery
categories.description: Understanding your tolerance for downtime helps us design the right level of resilience.

questions.text: If your primary systems were down, how long could your organization operate?

options.label: Minutes — immediate impact on operations or revenue
Hours — significant disruption but could manage briefly
A day — inconvenient but manageable
Multiple days — we could get by with workarounds

questions.text: How much data loss could you tolerate in a disaster scenario?

options.label: None — we need near-real-time protection
A few hours of data
Up to a day of data
We haven't thought about this

questions.text: Do you need your data replicated to a second physical location?

options.label: Yes — geographic redundancy is required
Yes — we'd prefer it for disaster protection
No — single-site is acceptable
Not sure

categories.title: Budget and Priorities
categories.description: Honest answers here help us recommend the right scope — not over-engineer or under-deliver.

questions.text: What is your approximate monthly budget for managed infrastructure services?

options.label: Under $200/month
Over $3,000/month
We need help scoping this

questions.text: Do you have existing server hardware, or would new equipment be needed?

options.label: We have a Raspberry Pi or an old laptop we could use
We have existing server or desktop hardware that might be usable
We'd need new equipment
Not sure what's needed

questions.text: What does success look like for you in six months?

options.label: Our infrastructure is documented, secured, and we know it works
We own our infrastructure and can operate independently of any vendor
We can demonstrate our security posture to clients or auditors
We stop worrying about outages and focus on our business
All of the above
